one_line_thesis: Global IT services compounder trading at a depressed multiple versus robust FY2025 free cash flow; Gen AI is both reinvention tailwind and margin risk — accumulate on math pending FCF normalization review.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Accenture is a global IT services compounder with durable scale, a managed-services mix shift, and heavy capital return ($8.3 billion returned in FY2025). FY2025 free cash flow per share is about $17.19; H1 FY2026 revenue grew 7% with Q2 bookings stabilizing. At ~$139 (July 2026), Lawrence synthesis implies 26.6% per year over 7 years on filing-based owner cash. Moat is stable (brand, client relationships, delivery scale) but not wide; partial dhando because AI could compress future margins even at today's price. Marvin proposes accumulate on math; [HUMAN REVIEW] whether FY2025 cash flow is...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

timeline.label: Cash moved +129.4% versus the prior period (5,004,469.0 to 11,478,729.0).
Q2 FY2026: revenue $18.0B (+8% USD); bookings $22.1B (+1% LC); EPS $2.93 — early bookings stabilization after FY2025 flat bookings
FY2025 results: revenue $69.7B (+7% LC); bookings $80.6B (−1% LC); OCF $11.47B; $8.3B returned to shareholders
Business optimization initiated Q4 FY2025: $615M charges for headcount reductions; adjusted operating margin 15.6%
Board adopted Julie Sweet chair/CEO compensation framework for FY2026 proxy cycle
Geographic reorganization: Latin America moved to Americas; Growth Markets renamed Asia Pacific (comparables restated)
Completed prior business optimization program ($438M FY2024 charges); FY2024 adjusted EPS $11.95
Announced $3B multi-year generative AI investment to build reinvention capabilities and capture enterprise AI spend
Julie Sweet became chair of the board (added to CEO role held since 2019)
Julie Sweet appointed chief executive officer, succeeding Pierre Nanterme
Accenture plc incorporated in Ireland; NYSE listing as ACN following 2001 IPO structure

industry.structure: Global IT and consulting services oligopoly: Accenture, IBM Consulting, Indian IT majors (TCS, Infosys), and Big Four advisory compete on scale, price, and specialization. Switching costs exist on multi-year transformations but not monopoly pricing.
industry.share_shift: Accenture maintains revenue leadership; managed services growing faster than consulting (9% vs 6% FY2025). Indian firms gain on cost; hyperscaler in-house services compete on cloud-native work.
industry.trend: Generative AI is simultaneously a demand catalyst (enterprise reinvention) and a supply threat (fewer billable hours per project). Clients shifting spend toward recurring managed services and AI-enabled operations.

top_claims.claim: Cash moved +129.4% versus the prior period (5,004,469.0 to 11,478,729.0).

Operating income growth moved from +11.1% to +6.5% YoY in the latest quarter. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Generative AI reduces billable hours faster than Accenture captures new reinvention revenue, compressing margins and free cash flow per share from the FY2025 base.
Bookings stagnation: FY2025 bookings −1% local currency; one quarter of stabilization is not a trend.
People business cyclicality: Enterprise IT budget cuts in recession reduce consulting faster than managed services offset.
